title: Announcements
content:
Announcements

Okan Homepage

Announcements

Project Number: 2025-1-TR01-KA131-HED-00031955 Erasmus+ Student Traineeship Mobility

27
						October

2025

Within the scope of Erasmus+ Key Action 1: Learning Mobility of Individuals (KA131) Program, students will be selected to undertake internships abroad during the 2025-2026 Academic Year. It is planned that 6 students (including Faculties, Institutes, and Vocational Schools) will be designated as Main Candidates for Student Internship Mobility in the 2025 Contract Period. Associate Degree students can apply only for internships. Undergraduate and Graduate students can apply for both study and internship programs. Applications will be made online; the application period will start on

17/11/2025

and end on

01/12/2025

at 23:59. Applications sent by post or delivered in person to the office will not be accepted.

Erasmus English Exams

Written exam: Will be held in the classrooms of      the Foreign Languages Coordination Office at Tuzla Campus.

Speaking exam: Will be held in the classrooms of      the Foreign Languages Coordination Office at Tuzla Campus; only students      who pass the written exam can participate.

Exam dates and times will be announced later on      the

Erasmus website

.

English exams taken in preparatory classes or      preparatory periods are not valid.

Applications will be evaluated according to the following criteria:

Evaluation Criteria:

Students from all faculties will be ranked      according to the general scoring system determined for Erasmus+ Internship      Mobility (academic success and language score).

Only the highest-scoring student from each      faculty will be included in the quota.

Quota Allocation:

If more than one student from a faculty ranks      within the top 6, only the highest-scoring student from that faculty will      be selected; other quotas will be allocated to the highest-scoring      students from other faculties.

To ensure fair distribution among faculties,      quotas will only be given to students ranked first from each faculty.

Important Notes:

Erasmus+ KA131 Internship Mobility is not a      foreign language learning program. The candidate must have sufficient      language proficiency to carry out their internship abroad without      problems.

Internship mobility procedures must be completed      by

31 July 2027

.

Internships can only be carried out in Erasmus      program countries.

Our university has decided to provide a grant for      a 2-month duration

.

Click to here Detailed information about the Inclusion Support provided for Erasmus+ students

Application Documents

Online form: To be completed via

TURNA PORTAL

; printed and signed by the student. Physical      submission of the form is not required. The Office may request it if      necessary.

Updated Student Certificate: Must be e-government      barcode or OIS e-signed.

Updated Transcript: Must be e-government barcode      or OIS e-signed; “sample” transcripts will not be accepted.

T.C. Identity Card: Both sides scanned, uploaded      as a single PDF file.

English Exam Result Document: Erasmus English      exam results conducted by our university’s Foreign Languages Coordination      Office (minimum 60/100) are valid. Students without a language certificate      should select “I want to take the university foreign language exam.” Valid      scores from YDS (last 5 years) and YÖKDİL exams, or other equivalency      exams recognized by ÖSYM, are also accepted according to the latest      conversion table published by ÖSYM.

Internship Invitation Letter (if any): +10 points      if uploaded during application. If the student does not attend the      internship, the grant will be transferred to another student. Finding an      internship place is the responsibility of the candidate student.

Official documents must be uploaded for children      of martyrs/veterans, disabled students, or those under      protection/care/shelter decisions.

The student must be full-time enrolled in any      formal education level (First Cycle: Undergraduate, Associate; Second      Cycle: Master’s; Third Cycle: Doctorate).

a. First-cycle students: minimum 2.20/4.00 cumulative GPA; second and      third-cycle students: minimum 2.50/4.00.

b. Students who have transitioned from an associate degree and whose      transcript has not yet been issued: minimum graduation GPA 2.20/4.00.

c. The total duration of Erasmus+ higher education mobility activities      must not exceed 12 months if previously used.

d. Applications must be submitted within the announced application dates.

Criteria

Weighted    Score

Academic   Achievement

50%   (out of 100 points)

Language   Level

50%   (out of 100 points)

Children   of martyrs and veterans

+15   points*

Disabled   students (with official documentation)

+10   points*

*Priority is given to children of veterans and martyrs, as specified in relevant laws and decrees. Supporting documents must be provided.

Additional points:

Submission of internship acceptance letter:                                              +10 points

Self or first-degree relatives receiving disaster aid from AFAD:             +10 points

Internships aimed at improving digital skills (DOTs):                          +5 points

Internship required by curriculum:                                                         +5 points

Previously participated in the same type of project (with or without grant): -10 points

Participation in mobility in the student’s home country:          -10 points

Selected but did not participate without withdrawing:           -10 points

Applying for both mobility types simultaneously:                   -10 points

Non-attendance at mobility-related meetings/trainings without excuse:       -5 points

Declared but missed the language exam without excuse:        -5 points

Evaluation Process:

Candidates      will be ranked according to Erasmus+ score: 50% GPA, 50% language score.

10 points      deducted for each previous mobility participation.

Results will      be evaluated by the Erasmus Selection Committee appointed by the Rectorate      and announced on the

Erasmus website

:

Disciplinary      action or failing courses does not prevent participation.

Application Steps:

Apply through

TURNA PORTAL

.

Print and      sign the online application form (photo included). Physical submission is      not required.

Obtain the      Updated Student Certificate (e-government barcode or signed and stamped by      Student Affairs).

Obtain the      Updated Transcript (signed and stamped; “sample” not accepted).

Upload T.C.      Identity Card (both sides as single PDF).

IMPORTANT:

Completion of applications and uploading correct documents is the responsibility of candidates. Late applications will not be considered.
